articles.title: ICE Agent Charged With Assault After Motorists Say He Brandished Gun
articles.summary: Minnesota prosecutors have spent weeks investigating the conduct of immigration agents who took part in an immigration crackdown in the Twin Cities. This is the first case they have brought.

articles.title: Minnesota has charged an ICE officer with assault for alleged actions during immigration surge
articles.summary: Hennepin County officials say these are the first charges filed against a federal immigration agent related to the crackdown that brought thousands of federal officers to the state. The widespread ope

articles.title: Trump nominates former Coast Guard doctor as CDC chief
articles.summary: The nomination comes after months of interim leadership at the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention.

articles.title: Trump says Israel and Lebanon have agreed to a ceasefire
articles.summary: A ceasefire in Lebanon was an Iranian condition for continuing talks with the U.S. to end that war.

articles.title: How the conflict in Iran is testing the U.S.-European alliance
articles.summary: NPR's Mary Louise Kelly speaks with correspondents Greg Myre and Rob Schmitz about how the Iran war is affecting the U.S. alliance with Europe.

articles.title: ICE Has Detained Marie-Thérèse Ross-Mahé, an 85-Year-Old Widow
articles.summary: After Marie-Thérèse Ross-Mahé’s husband died, an inheritance battle exploded. Her stepson then used his influence to have her arrested, an Alabama probate judge said.

articles.title: Judge Again Halts Aboveground Construction on Trump Ballroom
articles.summary: A federal judge wrote that an exception he made for work on security features did not cover most of the construction on the larger ballroom project Trump has proposed.

articles.title: House Votes to Preserve Deportation Protections for Haitians, Rebuking Trump
articles.summary: The action was largely symbolic since the president would be all but certain to veto the bill, but the bipartisan vote reflected resistance within his own ranks on his signature issue.

articles.title: A jury declared Live Nation a monopoly. But ticket prices won't drop just yet
articles.summary: D.C. and 33 states now have to argue in favor of specific remedies and fines, which could be paused if Live Nation appeals. Experts say the long-term impact on ticket prices isn't clear either.

articles.title: Panel Advances Trump’s Triumphal Arch, Even as Key Member Suggests Changes
articles.summary: The Commission of Fine Arts, a Trump-aligned advisory body, granted preliminary approval. But its vice chairman suggested losing statues atop the structure and other revisions before a final vote.

articles.title: The Costa Rican Mountain Town Offering Sanctuary to Families Deported by Trump
articles.summary: In a cloud forest village, a network of residents, foreigners and pacifist Quakers offered a precarious yet vital sanctuary for families expelled by the U.S. government.

articles.title: Israeli and Palestinian activists share a vision for peace in Gaza
articles.summary: Maoz Inon's parents were killed by Hamas in the Oct. 7 attacks. Aziz Abu Sarah's brother died after being tortured in an Israeli military prison. Their new book is The Future Is Peace.

articles.title: Boiling milk and worrying about the Iran war: A New Year dawns in Sri Lanka
articles.summary: In Sri Lanka, Buddhists and Hindus marked their New Year on Tuesday while a war thousands of miles away is making itself felt.

articles.title: A complex set of negotiations to end Israel's overlapping wars
articles.summary: Israel has agreed to a ceasefire in Lebanon, part of a complex web of Mideast negotiations, from Iran to Gaza.

articles.title: Pope Leo takes aim at  'handful of tyrants' spending billions on war amid tensions with Trump
articles.summary: Pope Leo XIV condemns "tyrants" fueling war with billions. His calls for global peace during his Africa trip come amid rising tensions with President Trump.

articles.title: RFK Jr. to Reform Health Panel That Determines Which Screenings Insurers Cover
articles.summary: The health secretary said he would reform the panel, which determines whether insurance will cover preventive services like mammograms and colonoscopies.

articles.title: RFK Jr. defends his health agenda and Trump's proposed budget cuts in hearing
articles.summary: In his first appearance this year, the health secretary is taking questions on his record on health, including his controversial moves on vaccines.

articles.title: Utah Becomes the New Center of U.S. Measles Cases
articles.summary: Nearly 600 people have been sickened across the state, which has seen an increase in vaccine exemptions among children in recent years.

articles.title: Iran War timeline continues to shift. And, jury rules Live Nation acted as monopoly
articles.summary: Trump continues to try to declare victory in Iran as the timeline for the war's end shifts. And, a jury determined that Live Nation acted as a monopoly and overcharged ticket buyers.

articles.title: Israel agrees to a ceasefire in its war with Hezbollah but will keep troops in Lebanon
articles.summary: The leaders of Israel and Lebanon have agreed on a 10-day ceasefire in Israel's fight against Iran-backed Hezbollah in Lebanon. But Hezbollah vows to resist a continued Israeli troop presence.

articles.title: Indonesia's capital of the future faces doubts in the present
articles.summary: Indonesia is racing to build a new capital, promising a greener, futuristic city. But many citizens aren't convinced it will live up to the vision.

articles.title: Dominion still has pending lawsuits against election deniers such as Rudy Giuliani and Sidney Powell
